turning_points.name: The Powerplay Collapse
turning_points.description: CSK lost Gaikwad, Mhatre, and Samson in the space of 10 balls. Duffy created immediate mayhem -- the top three gone for 30, and the game already in RCBs hands.

turning_points.name: The Sarfaraz Blitz Ends
turning_points.description: Sarfaraz Khan had single-handedly dragged CSK back with 50 off 25 balls. Krunal Pandyas stumping ended that spell and removed the one batter who could have made the target feasible.

turning_points.name: The Patidar-David Eruption
turning_points.description: After Padikkal departed at 151, Patidar and David detonated the death overs. 99 runs in 34 balls at SR 291 buried any hope CSK had of managing the total.

counterfactual_scenarios.title: What If Sarfaraz Had Survived?
counterfactual_scenarios.change: Sarfaraz Khan, who fell stumped at 77/4 (over 6.1), faces the ball again. He adds 30 more runs over the next 4 overs at his actual SR.
counterfactual_scenarios.original_outcome: CSK lost by 43 runs, scoring 207 all out
counterfactual_scenarios.counterfactual_outcome: CSK would have scored ~237 and lost by ~14 runs
counterfactual_scenarios.narrative:
Sarfaraz Khan was operating at light speed. At 77/3 heading into the back-end of the powerplay, there was a realistic path to glory. Then Pandya planted the seed of doubt, Jitesh read it, and a clean stumping ended the finest innings of the chase.

The arithmetic of the counterfactual is stark: had Sarfaraz survived to bat six more overs at his exact scoring rate, CSK cross 237. The margin shrinks from 43 to roughly 14. Victory is still beyond reach, but every bowler second-guesses their plans.

The real question is why Pandya bowled at Sarfaraz at all. A wider ball, a tossed-up slower one -- anything to keep him at the non-striker end.

counterfactual_scenarios.title: What If Tim David Had Been Dismissed at 151?
counterfactual_scenarios.change: Tim David arrives in over 14.2 but is caught at deep midwicket off the first ball he faces.
counterfactual_scenarios.original_outcome: RCB scored 250/3. Tim David hit 70* off 25 balls
counterfactual_scenarios.counterfactual_outcome: RCB would have scored approximately 205-215, making the match genuinely competitive
counterfactual_scenarios.narrative:
The scoreboard said 151/3 after 14 overs. Achievable, uncomfortable, but very much alive for CSK. Then Tim David walked in, and every calculation had to be done again.

Had David gone first ball -- RCB would have had Jitesh Sharma or Krunal Pandya coming next. Good players, but none match the David equation: 280 strike rate, ice nerves, zero sentimentality.

In the counterfactual RCB land at roughly 210. A 210 target at the Chinnaswamy, under lights, with Sarfaraz and Prashant Veer in the shed? That is a different kind of terror.

commentary_gems.text: Sarfaraz loses it in the lights, and David has turbocharged RCB to 250! Full on the pads. David walks down the track and flicks it to deep mid wicket.
bashed in short, and Kohli bites the dust! There is stunned silence at the Chinnaswamy!
Dube does the trick! Inspired bowling change, and Salt perishes! Salt has a wild swipe at it and shanks it a mile up.
leg stump out of the ground! Overton provides a bit of respite to CSK! Yorker speared in on leg stump.
Kamboj outwits David, and the stumps have been shattered -- but it is a no ball! David pre-empts another wide delivery and walks across.

game_changers.key_stat: 70* off 25 balls (SR 280) -- Player of the Match
game_changers.reason: David arrived at 151/3 and turned an already-big total into an untouchable one. His 99-run stand with Patidar off just 34 balls was the axle on which the match turned. No CSK bowling plan could contain him once he found his range in over 15.

On a ground where 250 was scored batting first, Duffy opened the CSK chase with a 4-over spell at 5.50 RPO. He removed Gaikwad in the very first over and came back to dismiss Samson at 30/3. Those two wickets at the top reshaped the match.

game_changers.key_stat: 50 off 25 balls (SR 200) -- the only man who made it uncomfortable
game_changers.reason: When CSK were 30/3 after 2.5 overs, Sarfaraz was the only player who could have made this chase real. His blitz brought CSK to 77 and had the win probability at 38%. The stumping that ended his innings took the match away almost as conclusively as Duffys opening salvo.

game_changers.key_stat: 48* off 19 balls (SR 252.63) -- the engine of the 99-run stand
game_changers.reason: Patidar set the tone that David then amplified. His aggressive approach from over 14 onwards ensured CSK never had a moment to breathe. The two unbroken partnerships he anchored with Padikkal and then David yielded 157 runs off just 56 balls.
